paper_title: Study of Epiphytic Diatoms in the Szigetköz Region of the Danube

methods.method_name: Sampling Design for Monitoring Long-Term Changes
methods.description: This method describes the sampling design used to monitor long-term changes in epiphytic diatoms in the Szigetköz region of the Danube. It involves deploying floating reed islands to simulate natural conditions and collect samples over time.

steps.description: Deploy 'floating reed islands' at two sites in the Szigetköz region.
steps.searchable_summary: deploy floating reed islands for diatom sampling

steps.description: Collect samples weekly from reed islands at 10-20 cm intervals below the water surface from June to September.
steps.searchable_summary: collect weekly diatom samples from reed islands

steps.description: Collect control samples from rooting reeds near the reed island twice a year.
steps.searchable_summary: collect control diatom samples from natural reeds

steps.description: Collect reed epiphytic diatoms at sample points twice in July 2019.
steps.searchable_summary: collect diatom samples at specific points in July 2019

methods.method_name: Diatom Index and Trait Data Analysis
methods.description: This method involves calculating the Specific Pollution Sensitivity Index (IPS) and analyzing diatom traits to assess the ecological potential of branch systems.

steps.description: Calculate the Specific Pollution Sensitivity Index (IPS) using OMNIDIA 6.0.2.
Classify diatom species into ecological guilds and other traits using existing databases.
steps.searchable_summary: classify diatoms into ecological guilds and traits

steps.description: Determine top, core, and common species based on abundance and presence in samples.
steps.searchable_summary: identify top, core, and common diatom species

methods.description: This method uses a linear regression model to estimate the relative abundance of diatom traits during periods without monitoring and assesses the impact of rehabilitation on the ecosystem.

steps.description: Use a linear regression model (GLM) to estimate relative abundance of diatom traits during unmonitored periods.
steps.searchable_summary: apply GLM to estimate diatom abundance

steps.description: Validate the model with data from samples collected in 2019.
steps.searchable_summary: validate GLM model with 2019 sample data

steps.description: Estimate the IPS diatom index for the missing period using the model.
steps.searchable_summary: estimate IPS index using GLM model
